title: Less Than Hero

jokes.text: Your call is being-- Connected. By SewerCom. Reach out and touch the sewers.
jokes.punchline: Twist on AT&T's 'Reach out and touch someone' for sewer mutants
jokes.explanation: Clever corporate parody that establishes the mutant sewer setting with economical wordplay

jokes.text: Dad, relax. You are being so mutant.
jokes.punchline: Using 'mutant' as a generational put-down
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Leela treats being a mutant like a typical teen treats being old

jokes.text: Because they're inferior genetic scum. Present company excluded, of course.
jokes.punchline: Perfunctory politeness after the insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - casual cruelty followed by hollow social niceties

jokes.text: While you're there, could you get me a license to kill? Sure. Bare hands or weapon? What does piano wire count as?
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact response about method specifics
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that treats murder bureaucratically, with Leela's deadpan government worker response

jokes.text: Enjoy your affordable Swedish crap.
jokes.setup: IKEA parody Pi-Kea robot delivering furniture
jokes.punchline: Blunt honesty about product quality
jokes.explanation: Sharp IKEA parody with robot's brutal honesty about cheap furniture

jokes.text: There. Finished. And only six missing pieces.
jokes.punchline: Admission that significant parts are missing
jokes.explanation: Relatable IKEA assembly frustration with Professor's typical incompetence

jokes.text: I feel like I was mauled by Jesus.
jokes.setup: Fry's pain comparison to fighting Thor
jokes.punchline: Bender's blasphemous escalation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from Fry's mythological reference to Bender's casually blasphemous one-up

jokes.text: I feel like I was mauled by Jesus.
jokes.punchline: More absurd and controversial comparison - Jesus as violent
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that's both shocking and perfectly in character for Bender - economical and memorable

jokes.text: Two hours later, he was gone with 60 of my dollars! But I had the miracle cream.
jokes.setup: Professor's entire snake oil salesman story
jokes.punchline: Treating obvious scam as successful transaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor character moment - his gullibility presented as triumph

jokes.text: Hand over your wallets! I don't believe that story for a second. It doesn't matter, I'm mugging you! There's no bus to Jersey City.
jokes.punchline: Fry fact-checking the mugging excuse
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment where Fry treats a mugging like a customer service interaction

jokes.text: I'm too scared to find my pocket. Here, I'll just take off my pants and give you those.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre overcompensation solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry logic - turning a simple request into an unnecessarily complicated solution

jokes.text: Give it to him again, Andrew!
jokes.punchline: Bender cheering for more violence with unexpected personal name
jokes.explanation: Bender's bloodthirsty encouragement with the unexpected intimacy of using a name

jokes.text: Give it to him again, Andrew!
jokes.punchline: Implying whatever Fry did worked and she wants him to repeat it
jokes.explanation: Continues the gag efficiently - Leela's now encouraging whatever weird thing Fry did

jokes.text: Foiled. And after years of planning. You're out of the gang.
jokes.punchline: Treating mugging like elaborate heist with employment consequences
jokes.explanation: Absurd formality applied to petty crime with workplace dynamics

jokes.text: Maybe we're wearing magic rings, but they're invisible so we don't even realize it. Also, you can't feel the rings.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate unfalsifiable theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - creating an unfalsifiable theory by removing all evidence

jokes.text: You'd think you'd have to freebase it.
jokes.punchline: Casual drug reference for enhanced absorption
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating super powers like recreational drugs

jokes.text: Hey, Zoidberg, get in here! Screw you! Ain't got that. Nope.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's typical hostile response
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg moment - hostile rejection when called upon

jokes.text: I'll be able to pack my day with twice as many humdrum activities.
jokes.punchline: Using them for mundane efficiency
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - super powers used for tedious optimization rather than heroism

jokes.text: To have parents. Whatever.
jokes.punchline: Leela's tragic backstory dismissed casually
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor - Leela's tragic orphan status treated as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: That incorporates my violence.
jokes.setup: Looking for ways to serve community
jokes.punchline: Violence as a community service requirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela - civic duty combined with her violent tendencies

jokes.text: You say you're a duo? Yeah, duos are good. Of course, sometimes they're a little short-Handed. See you.
jokes.setup: Bender commenting on the duo formation
jokes.punchline: Obvious hint that duos need a third member, fake casual exit
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Bender joining the team - transparent manipulation that's funny because it's so obvious

jokes.text: With two humans, you'd think there'd be a robot in there to balance things out. But whatever.
jokes.punchline: Bender's passive-aggressive self-insertion
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender manipulation - playing victim to get included

jokes.text: I have these three costumes you could use, but I guess I'll just throw one away.
jokes.setup: Bender's continued campaign to join the team
jokes.punchline: Most obvious manipulation yet - having exactly three costumes ready
jokes.explanation: Peak of the manipulation sequence - having exactly three costumes makes his planning hilariously obvious

jokes.text: Also, I got this going: [mechanical sound]
jokes.punchline: Presumably ridiculous mechanical display
jokes.explanation: Visual gag relying on Bender's mechanical body for physical comedy

jokes.text: Quiet! It's 4 a.m and I just fell asleep for the first time in 30 years! Sorry!
jokes.punchline: Extreme insomnia as mundane complaint
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of a normal complaint to impossible extremes

jokes.text: And Super King! The best one of the three!
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately claiming superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego moment - can't help but promote himself during team introduction

jokes.text: Sure! I'm listed in the phone book, so once I reveal my real name... Along with my address and a copy of my birth certificate--
jokes.punchline: Fry offering increasingly comprehensive personal information
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry oversharing - turning a date into identity theft vulnerability

jokes.text: Super King has no need for loved ones.
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual dismissal of emotional attachment
jokes.explanation: Bender's robotic detachment presented as superhero virtue

jokes.text: That's fantastic news! Because I get to summon the New Justice Team!
jokes.punchline: Excitement about getting to use superheroes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - politician excited about crisis as PR opportunity

jokes.text: Oh, I completely forgot! I left my apartment on fire! As for me, I'm late for my LSATs. And I can't take life anymore!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic and implausible excuses
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity from mundane to suicidal as excuses

jokes.text: As for me, I'm late for my LSATs.
jokes.punchline: Leela's more mundane but equally sudden excuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation sequence with Leela's more realistic but equally sudden excuse

jokes.text: A badger with a troubled past and nothing left to lose.
jokes.punchline: Action movie cliché applied to badger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant application of action movie character archetype to woodland creature

jokes.text: An elephant who never forgets to kill.
jokes.punchline: Dark twist on elephant memory saying
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay - perfect economy turning innocent saying into threat

jokes.text: A seldom-used crab named Lucky, a.k.a. Citizen Snips.
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic naming convention for minor criminal crab
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with pun on Citizen Kane for a crab

jokes.text: Nine, ten, a big fat hen. The name's Bender.
jokes.punchline: Bender's non sequitur introduction
jokes.explanation: Random Bender moment - inserting himself into unrelated conversation

jokes.text: Relax. I'm sure she's just ashamed of us.
jokes.setup: Concern about Leela not showing up
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of parental shame
jokes.explanation: Dark parent humor - treating shame as the most logical explanation

jokes.text: I don't know. I never heard of no mayor.
jokes.punchline: Cop's ignorance of basic government
jokes.explanation: Standard incompetent authority figure joke

jokes.text: Man boxing a kangaroo is a peculiar spectacle. But a kangaroo boxing a robot? Now I'm afraid you've lost me.
jokes.punchline: Line drawn at robot vs kangaroo despite accepting human vs kangaroo
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdity - arbitrary line between acceptable and unacceptable weirdness

jokes.text: I'm scared and confused. I think we wandered into an off-Broadway play. No, there are way too many people here.
jokes.punchline: Off-Broadway attendance burn
jokes.explanation: Sharp theater industry joke with perfect setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: I'm scared and confused. I think we wandered into an off-Broadway play.
jokes.punchline: Comparing the situation to experimental theater
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the absurdity of the situation resembling avant-garde theater

jokes.text: No, there are way too many people here.
jokes.punchline: Rejecting the comparison because off-Broadway has small audiences
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-up that shows knowledge of theater while maintaining the absurd logic

jokes.text: Fool! A quip about putting me behind bars would have been far more delicious!
jokes.setup: Hero's threat to put him in captivity
jokes.punchline: Villain critiquing missed pun opportunity
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy gold - villain as comedy critic during his own defeat

jokes.text: Yet the Zookeeper escaped, thus proving that the deadliest animal of all... Is the Zookeeper.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Anti-climactic wisdom that says nothing while sounding profound

jokes.text: It's good that Leela doesn't love us. She'll be less sad when we die.
jokes.punchline: Morbid silver lining logic
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny parent logic - finding upside in emotional distance through mortality

jokes.text: And I got the audio tour for Treasures of Liberace's Tomb!
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific museum exhibit
jokes.explanation: Absurd future museum exhibit combining morbidity with kitsch

jokes.text: Oh, I can't take it! Mom, Dad, I'm Clobberella!
jokes.setup: Leela's guilt about disappointing her parents
jokes.punchline: Dramatic secret identity revelation as emotional release
jokes.explanation: Important plot moment that works as comedy through the dramatic revelation trope

jokes.text: Well, that's wonderful! But did you have to make the costume so revealing?
jokes.setup: Mother learning daughter is a superhero
jokes.punchline: Immediately focusing on parental concern about revealing costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental response - pride mixed with typical mother's modesty concerns

jokes.text: Look at me! Proud dad of a superhero! We should print up T-Shirts! And F-Shirts for our friends with two arms on the same side!
jokes.punchline: Wanting to make shirts including ones for anatomically unusual mutant friends
jokes.explanation: Great world-building joke that shows the father's enthusiasm while establishing mutant diversity

jokes.text: And F-Shirts for our friends with two arms on the same side!
jokes.punchline: Accommodating mutant anatomy
jokes.explanation: Clever world-building joke about mutant diversity and inclusive merchandising

jokes.text: Dad, you drink? No, not even then!
jokes.setup: Morris asking about drinking and telling secrets
jokes.punchline: Leela surprised by drinking revelation, Morris clarifying abstinence
jokes.explanation: Mild character revelation humor about family drinking habits

jokes.text: All right. My sucker is sealed.
jokes.punchline: Saying 'sucker' instead of lips - both a mispronunciation and literal description
jokes.explanation: Good character wordplay that suggests both speech impediment and literal mutant anatomy

jokes.text: You're pulling me! Keep it under your sock.
jokes.punchline: Sock-based secrecy for mutant anatomy
jokes.explanation: Mutation-appropriate version of 'keep it under your hat'

jokes.text: Leela's parents blabbed. Leela? Is the person that parrot is mimicking telling the truth by proxy?
jokes.punchline: Formal interrogation of parrot testimony
jokes.explanation: Absurd legal formality applied to parrot hearsay evidence

jokes.text: Thank you very much, Morris!
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic blame assignment
jokes.explanation: Standard marital blame humor in crisis situation

jokes.text: Oh, God! Every couch and table has animal fur all over it. He lets them climb on everything!
jokes.setup: Captured by villain with trained animals
jokes.punchline: Focusing on pet hygiene concerns
jokes.explanation: Priorities humor - housekeeping complaints during life-threatening situation

jokes.text: Or your parents will be devoured by piranhas that I have, shall we say... Persuaded to walk on dry land.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of training piranhas to be land-based
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist threat with the polite euphemism 'persuaded' for impossible animal training

jokes.text: Save us, Leela! I mean, if you don't have anything better to do!
jokes.punchline: Polite qualification of emergency request
jokes.explanation: Sweet parent humor - being considerate even while kidnapped

jokes.text: The thought of stealing anything fills Super King with disgust. But if it must be so, then let a museum heist be pulled!
jokes.punchline: Bender's fake reluctance and dramatic acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - theatrical reluctance about his favorite activity

jokes.text: Check inside the cap. There might be a caked-in gold mine. El Zilcho.
jokes.setup: Looking for remaining super power cream
jokes.punchline: Optimistic drug residue hope followed by Spanish disappointment
jokes.explanation: Relatable desperation behavior with bilingual disappointment

jokes.text: El Zilcho. I'm afraid we're boned.
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual Spanish mixed with his catchphrase about being in trouble
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender catchphrase with Spanish flourish - functional character moment

jokes.text: Can l, like, wail on you with this two-By-Four... And it doesn't hurt you? Ow! That didn't hurt.
jokes.setup: Person testing if Fry still has super powers
jokes.punchline: Fry claiming it didn't hurt immediately after crying out in pain
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Fry's transparent lie about not feeling pain

jokes.text: Ow! That didn't hurt.
jokes.setup: Testing if super powers still work
jokes.punchline: Contradictory pain response
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke about maintaining superhero facade

jokes.text: Stay back, or we'll beat the tar out of you using super powers! Whoo-ooah.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic display of powerlessness
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of weak threat followed by obvious impotence

jokes.text: Stay back, or we'll beat the tar out of you using super powers!
jokes.punchline: Claiming they'll use super powers they no longer have
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony with characters bluffing about abilities they've lost

jokes.text: You think I'd show you my lair? My lair's a million times nicer than this.
jokes.punchline: Defensive pride about superior evil headquarters
jokes.explanation: Villain vanity humor - evil mastermind worried about real estate reputation

jokes.text: We're just happy to be involved in your life.
jokes.setup: Leela apologizing for getting them kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Dad's pathetically grateful reaction to criminal endangerment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly heartbreaking - dad so desperate for connection he's grateful for kidnapping, great character work

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Just like it's natural for parents... To be a little disappointed in their children. Not that we are.
jokes.punchline: Immediate backpedaling qualification
jokes.explanation: Sweet parent diplomacy - universal truth with personal exception

jokes.text: Just like it's natural for parents... To be a little disappointed in their children. Not that we are.
jokes.punchline: Immediate qualification that they're not disappointed, suggesting they might be
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with mother's awkward honesty about parent-child relationships

jokes.text: Hey, Fry, we've still got our costumes on. Wanna steal some more stuff? I guess. As long as you think it's right.
jokes.punchline: Fry's continued moral deference to Bender about crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Fry's moral compass permanently broken by following Bender

tmdb_overview: When Fry and Leela discover a cream that gives them unusual powers, they form a superhero team along with Bender.
